name: La Cite du Vin
summary: Major wine culture museum with tasting at the Belvedere included in the permanent exhibition ticket.
whyItMatters: A reliable daytime Bordeaux wine anchor if bars are crowded or race-day city-center logistics get messy.
pros: Official page lists daily 10:00-19:00 opening from Apr 3 to Oct 31, 2026.
Permanent exhibition ticket includes a tasting in the Belvedere.
Official access notes identify tram line B stop La Cite du Vin as a two-minute walk.
cons: Museum visit can take more time than a casual bar stop.
Not in the historic center; needs tram planning.

logistics.opening_hours: Apr 3-Oct 31, 2026: open every day 10:00-19:00 per official page.
logistics.reservation_needed: Date-stamped e-ticket recommended; official page links to ticketing.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Likely away from central finish crowds, but verify against final viewing point.
logistics.transit: Official page says tram line B, stop La Cite du Vin, is a two-minute walk.
logistics.parking: Official page notes nearby paid parking, but tram is the safer default.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Verify TBM and city notices for Stage 7 before relying on tram timings.

timing.best_visit_window: Late morning or afternoon on a non-race day; on race day only if transit is confirmed.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium for Fri Jul 10, 2026 because tram/city access may change.

sources.title: La Cite du Vin - Opening hours, access and prices
sources.note: Supports 2026 opening hours, address/access details, tram line B stop, tickets, and the Belvedere tasting included with the permanent exhibition ticket.

open_questions: Check whether special Tour de France programming or closures are announced for the Bordeaux race week.
Confirm whether the group wants a full museum visit or only a wine-bar style stop.
